pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This is a longish 'a' sound, like you would hear in the words 'alms' or 'father.' Note that you will not hear a sound similar to the first 'a' in the word 'animal.' So, the word 'Άλογο' is pronounced 'ah-loh-ghoh.'
Although it looks a lot like a 'b,' the βήτα is pronounced very much like the English 'v,' and in fact, the letter itself is pronounced 'vee-tah.' So, the word 'Βουβάλι' is pronounced 'voo-vah-lee.'
Gamma is a tricky one! It's not a hard 'g,' but neither is it a soft 'h.' Rather, it seems to be halfway between the two, as a soft, gargled 'gh.' 'Γάτα' is pronounced 'ghah-tah.'

Despite looking like an English 'd,' the delta is pronounced like the 'th' sound in the words 'these' and 'those.' (It is not, however, pronounced like the softer 'th' in 'bath' or 'death.') So, 'Διαβάζω' is pronounced 'thee-ah-vah-zoe.'
Epsilon sounds like a short 'e' in English, meaning it's similar in sound to the 'e' in 'held' or 'pen,' but not the longer 'e' as in 'evil' or 'emit. 'Έχω' is pronounced 'eh-ho.'
Zeta is pronounced like the English 'z.' So, the word 'Ζωη' is pronounced 'zoh-ee.'

alphabetItems.exampleWord: Κρήτη, the name of the island we call 'Crete'

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: Eta is pronounced like the 'ee' in 'feet' or greet.' This means that 'Κρήτη' is pronounced 'kree-tee.'
The theta is pronounced like a soft 'th,' as in 'path' or 'mathematics.' 'Θέμα' is pronounced 'theh-mah.'
Iota sounds like a long i (meaning the letter itself is pronounced like 'yota'). It sounds like the 'i' sound in 'machine,' meaning 'κίτρινα' is pronounced 'ki-tri-nah.'
Kappa is identical to the sound an English 'k' makes. So, 'Κορίτσι' is pronounced 'koh-ree-tsee.'
Lambda is pronounced like the English 'l,' which means that the word 'Λέει' is pronounced 'lay-ee.'
Mu is pronounced like the English 'm,' meaning that 'Μαμἀ' is pronounced 'ma-ma.'
Nu has a pronunciation identical to the English 'n.' 'Πίνουν' is pronounced 'pee-noon.'
Xi is pronounced like the English letter 'x,' although it can be used at any point in the word, including the beginning, without changing its pronunciation. So, 'Ξένος' is pronounced 'kseh-nos.'

Omicron is very similar to, but a softer 'o' sound, than the long 'o' of an omega. They are very similar, but when an omicron is found at the end of a word, the word tends to be a noun, adjective, or article, but not a verb. 'Όνομα' is pronounced 'oh-no-mah.' (Because of the accent, the first omicron is breathy.)
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a soft, long 'o' that ends many adjectives, articles, and nouns

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: Pi is pronounced like the English 'p' (and the letter itself is pronounced like 'pee'), which means that 'Πόσο' is pronounced 'po-so.'

Rho's pronunciation is similar to the English 'r,' but it is slightly trilled, similiar to how an 'r' is pronounced in Spanish or Italian. Make sure your tongue hits the roof of your mouth when you say the rho in 'ρόδα,' which is pronounced '´roh-tha.'
Sigma is pronounced like the English 's.' So, the word 'σκούρος,' which means 'dark,' is pronounced with the same 's' sound as its English counterpart. Say it 'skoo-ros.'
Tau is pronounced like an English 't.' The word 'Τι' is pronounced as it's spelled: 'ti.'

Ypsilon is a 'ee' sound, like the vowel version of 'y.' For example, the 'y' sound in 'candy.' The accent on the leading vowel adds a breathy quality, and you'll often see it transliterated with a preceding 'h.' Thus, 'Ύμνος' is pronounced like 'heem-nos.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a long 'ee' sound like the vowel 'y' in 'candy'

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: Phi is pronounced like the English 'f.' 'Ελέφαντας' is pronounced 'eh-leh-fan-tas.'
Chi (in Greek, the letter is pronounced like 'hee'), is pronounced much like the English 'h.' So, the word 'όχι' sounds like 'oh-hee.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: an 'h,' as in 'how,' or the 'h' in 'behave'

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: True to its name, the letter Ψ is prounced as a combination of the consonant sounds of 'p' and 's,' and it can be found in any place in the word, including the very beginning. It sounds the same as the 'ps' combination in the English words 'claps' or 'traps,' so 'Ψυχή' is pronounced 'psee-hee.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a combination of the consonant sounds 'p' and 's,' forming a 'ps' as in 'cyclops'

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: Omega is a long 'o' sound, as in 'over' or 'post.' Shorter 'o' sounds are represented by a separate letter, the omicron. When an omega is found at the end of a word, that word tends to be a verb. The word 'Ωκεανός' is pronounced 'oh-kay-ah-nos.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a long 'o' that ends many verbs

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This is a longish 'a' sound, like you would hear in the words 'alms' or 'father.' Note that you will not hear a sound similar to the first 'a' in the word 'animal.' The word 'γράμμα' is pronounced 'hrah-mah.'

Although it looks a lot like a 'b,' the βήτα is pronounced very much like the English 'v,' and in fact, the letter itself is pronounced 'vee-tah.' Therefore, the word 'βιβλίο' is pronounced 'vee-vlee-oh.'

Gamma is a tricky one! It's not a hard 'g,' but neither is it a soft 'h.' Rather, it seems to halfway between the two, as a soft, gargled 'gh.' Therefore, 'βιολογία' is pronounced 'vee-oh-lo-ghee-ah.'

Despite looking like an English 'd,' the delta is pronounced like the 'th' sound in the words 'these' and 'those.' (It is not, however, pronounced like the softer 'th' in 'bath' or 'death.') So, 'διαμάντι' is pronounced 'thee-ah-mahn-tee.'
Epsilon sounds like a short 'e' in English, meaning it's similar in sound to the 'e' in 'held' or 'pen,' but not the longer 'e' as in 'evil' or 'emit. 'Μέση' is pronounced 'meh-see.'
Zeta is pronounced like the English 'z.' So, the word 'ζουζούνι' is pronounced like 'zoo-zoo-nee.'
Eta is pronounced like the 'ee' in 'feet' or greet.' This means that 'αράχνη' is pronounced 'ah-rah-hnee.'
The theta is pronounced like a soft 'th,' as in 'path' or 'mathematics' (not a coincidence), meaning that 'μαθηματικά' is pronounced, as you would expect, 'mah-thee-mah-tee-kah.'
Iota sounds like a long i (meaning the letter itself is pronounced like 'yota'). It sounds like the 'i' sound in 'machine,' meaning 'κόκκινη' is pronounced 'koh-ki-nee.'
Kappa is identical to the sound an English 'k' makes. So, 'πορτοκαλί' is pronounced 'por-toe-kah-lee.'
Lambda is pronounced like the English 'l,' which means that the word 'καλό' is pronounced 'kah-low.'
Mu is pronounced like the English 'm,' meaning that 'ομόνοια' is pronounced 'oh-moe-nee-ah'
Nu has a pronunciation identical to the English 'n.' 'Νερό' is pronounced 'neh-roe.'
Xi is pronounced like the English letter 'x,' and it can be used at any point in the word, including the beginning, without changing its pronunciation. 'Λεξικό' is pronounced 'leks-ih-ko.'

Omicron is a softer 'o' sound than the long 'o' of an omega. This is easy to remember, since the 'o' sound is similar to the English word 'soft.' Note, however, that the letter becomes like a hard 'o' if it ends the word. Because the omicron ends the word 'φρέσκο,' it is pronounced 'fres-koh.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a soft, long 'o' that ends many adjectives, articles, and nouns

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: Pi is pronounced like the English 'p' (and the letter itself is pronounced like 'pee'), which means 'πάπια' is pronounced 'pah-pee-ah.'

Rho's pronunciation is similar to the English 'r,' but it is slightly trilled, similiar to how an 'r' is pronounced in Spanish or Italian. Make sure your tongue hits the roof of your mouth when you reach the rho in 'παρακαλώ,' which is pronounced 'pah-´rah-kah-lo.'
Sigma is pronounced like the English 's.' So, the word 'στάση,' which means 'stop,' is pronounced with the same 's' sound as its English counterpart. Say it 'stah-see.'
Sigma is pronounced like the English 's.' So, the word 'γάτες,' which means 'cats,' is pronounced with the same 's' sound as its English counterpart. Say it 'ghah-tes.'
Tau is pronounced like an English 't.' The word 'τρώει' is pronounced 'troh-ee.'
Ypsilon is a 'ee' sound, or a long 'i' as in 'machine.' So, 'σκύλος' is pronounced like 'skee-los.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a long 'ee' sound like the vowel 'y' in 'candy'

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: Phi is pronounced like the English 'f.' 'καφέ' is pronounced 'kah-feh.'
Chi (in Greek, the letter is pronounced like 'hee,' is pronounced much like the English 'h,' meaning that 'χύμος' is pronounced 'hee-mos.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: an 'h,' as in 'how,' or the 'h' in 'behave'

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: The letter Ψ is prounced as a combination of the consonant sounds of 'p' and 's,' and it can be found in any place in the word, including the very beginning. It sounds the same as the 'ps' combination in the English words 'claps' or 'traps,' so 'ψάρι' is pronounced 'psah-ree.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a combination of the consonant sounds 'p' and 's,' forming a 'ps' as in 'cyclops'

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: Omega is a long 'o' sound, as in 'over' or 'post.' Shorter 'o' sounds are represented by a separate letter, the omicron. The word 'πρώτα' is pronounced 'pro-tah.'
pronunciations.modernShortPronunciation: a long 'o' that ends many verbs

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination has a short-e sound, like 'eh.' So, 'παιδί' sounds like 'peh-di.

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the vowels alpha (α) and iota (ι).

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination has an 'ahf' sound, with a distinct 'f' at the end. Thus, 'αυτό' sounds like 'af-to.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the vowels alpha (α) and ypsilon (υ).

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination makes a sound like 'ee' or the long 'i' in 'bring,' meaning 'εκείνοι' is pronounced 'eh-kee-nee.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the vowels epsilon (ε) and iota (ι).

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination has an 'eff' sound, with a distinct 'f' at the end despite not containing a φ. Thus, 'ευχαριστώ' sounds like 'eff-har-is-to.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the vowels epsilon (ε) and ypsilon (υ).

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination makes a sound like 'ee.' 'Όλοι' is pronounced 'oh-lee.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the vowels omicron (ο) and iota (ι).

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination sounds like a long 'oo,' like the English words 'school' or 'pool.' 'Σκούρο' is pronoucned 'skoo-ro.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the vowels omicron (ο) and ypsilon (υ).

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination sounds like an English 'ng' as in 'sing' or the word 'English' itself. So, 'Αγγλικά' is pronounced 'ang-lee-ka.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of two gammas (γγ), but it has a sound different from a single gamma.

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination adds a soft 'ng' sound with a breathy 'gh' sounds like that of the χ itself. So 'άγχος' is pronounced 'ang-hohs.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the consonants gamma and chi, but although the chi sound shines through at the end, the combination creates an 'ng' sound as well.

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: While the beta would seem like a good candidate to have a 'b' sound, it really acts as a 'v,' leaving this consonant cluster to sound like the English 'b.' Thus, μπαμπάς sounds like 'ba-bas.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the consonants mu and pi, but sounds unlike either one alone.

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination sounds very much like an English 'd.' Given that the delta is more like a hard 'th,' this consonant cluster takes the place of the 'd' sound. 'Αντίο' is pronounced very much like the French 'adieu,' like 'ah-dee-oh.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the consonants nu and tau, but sounds unlike either one alone.

pronunciations.modernPronunciation: This combination sounds like the English 'ch' sound, as in 'touch.' 'Τσάι' is pronounced like 'chay.'

alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of the consonants tau and sigma, but it sounds unlike either one alone.
